## [18:33 - 21:34]

It's what we want to do. In fact, I tend to actually take elements out of the main product and put them into the bonuses, because if the only time they hear about it is while I'm describing the course, they actually tend to place less value on that and attention to it than they do if I carve it out into the bonus section, especially if I use the bonus system that I described to you here. Now, I'm going to use an example, which is not an exact duplicate, because you actually have to pay for accessories in a car, but stick with me for just a moment, because I think you'll see the point I'm trying to make here. When you buy a car, you attend to assume that the car part, the motor seats, that kind of stuff, is part, that part of it is going to be great. You assume I'm buying a great car. And so what you tend to do is you start focusing on the other accessories. Okay. So basically, as I go from one car to another on the same lot or one lot to another or wherever, I'm assuming I'm getting a car that's going to work and it's going to work for a long time and not break. And so then I start focusing on things like heated leather seats and how it connects with my mobile devices and the legroom in the back, because I'm 6'4", and I want to have legroom for my kids who are also tall and all those different elements. And what ends up happening is the cell is made with the accessories, not the car itself. Again, because I assume the car itself is going to be a great car. And that is a natural human behavior. If I'm buying detergent, I'm assuming my clothes are getting clean. So then if I put Color Safe Bleach in there, oh, it's going to make them brighter. Oh, that's a bonus. It's something on top of the basic. So all this is a natural human behavior and the best selling webinars capitalize on this natural human behavior. In fact, they often spend five minutes talking about the product itself, describing what's in the product, and 20 to 45 minutes talking about the bonuses. I really want to stress this to you, because this is exactly the way I structure my webinars. About five minutes going through what's in the product, and then you're going to hear 20 to 45 minutes talking about the bonuses.
